title: Midday: Oil Plunges Below $90 on Iran Peace Push, Stocks Rally
commentary: Crude collapses 4.4% to $88.32 as US cease-fire proposal with Iran gains traction—biggest intraday drop in weeks sends energy longs scrambling. Dow +265 to 46,391, S&P +0.5%, Nasdaq +0.85% as risk appetite returns across the board. Precious metals surge on relief rally: gold +3.8% to $4,546, silver +5.3% to $73—outperforming despite fading safe-haven demand. BTC steady at $71,698 with $14B options expiry Friday eyed as price magnet to $75K. KB Home cuts guidance citing war impact on housing; Merck's $6.7B cancer buyout lifts healthcare sentiment.

title: US Pre-Market: Oil Plunges 6% on Iran Peace Push, Stocks Set for Rally
commentary: Crude nosedives 6%+ to $86.80 as US sends Iran a 15-point peace proposal—biggest de-escalation signal yet sparks risk-on rotation across all asset classes. Dow futures point to 500+ gain, S&P/Nasdaq set for gap-up as energy-driven inflation fears ease temporarily. Gold and silver catch bid (+1.6%, +2.2%) on relief rally despite softening safe-haven demand. BTC +1.7% to $71,714, ETH +1.4% to $2,186—crypto tracking equities higher. All eyes on Tehran's response; Fed's Barr warns rates may stay elevated on sticky inflation.

title: Market Update
commentary: Markets trading in mixed fashion as investors digest overnight developments. Risk assets showing resilience despite macro uncertainties. Key levels being tested across equity indices while commodities consolidate recent moves.

title: Asia Late Session: Markets Consolidate After Midday Volatility
commentary: Asian trading winds down with modest losses as oil's midday surge fades—Nikkei -0.3%, HSI -0.6% after earlier Iran-driven swings. WTI stabilizes near $91 as traders digest the ceasefire pause timeline; Brent holds $102. Gold drifts at $4,425 as safe-haven flows pause; crypto quiet with BTC at $70K, ETH $2,135. European open will test whether Tuesday's defensive rotation holds or tech weakness spreads. Iran's 48-hour response window remains the single variable driving all asset class correlations.

title: Midday Update: Oil Surges 4% as Dow Turns Green, Tech Lags
commentary: WTI crude punches to $91.70 (+4.0%) as Iran war jitters return—Brent comfortably above $100 at $103.08 keeps energy sector bid. Dow edges positive (+0.1%) on defensive rotation while Nasdaq (-0.5%) underperforms as growth stocks face profit-taking. Gold holds $4,428 (+0.5%), USD steady at 99.38 as traders await next Iran headline. BTC dips below $70K to $69,917 (-1.4%), ETH -0.9% at $2,133—crypto tracking macro risk, not leading.

title: Midday Update: Oil Spikes 4.4% as Tech Drags, Dow Outperforms
commentary: Oil punches back above $92 (+4.4%) in the session's biggest mover, reigniting supply-side jitters as Iran's strike-pause deadline approaches—Brent likely above $100 again. Nasdaq underperforms at -0.69% as growth names face renewed pressure; Dow ekes out gains at +0.17% on defensive rotation. Precious metals flat (gold $4,405, silver $69.67) as traders await clarity on geopolitical front. BTC steady at $69,899, ETH at $2,137—crypto content to track macro rather than lead.

title: Tuesday Trading: Stocks Retreat as Oil Rebounds, War Uncertainty Lingers
commentary: Wall Street gives back Monday's de-escalation rally as Brent crude punches through $100 and Iran war uncertainty returns to the fore—Dow -0.6% at 45,921, Nasdaq -0.6% at 24,036. Asia outperforms with HSI +2.8%, Nikkei +1.4% on spillover optimism that hasn't crossed the Pacific. Euro zone PMIs flash stagflation warnings as energy crunch bites; Treasury yields climb. Bitcoin slides to $70,079 (-1.2%), Ethereum -0.6% at $2,139 as risk appetite sours. One-variable market: it's all Iran now.

title: Asia-Pacific Open: De-escalation Rally Extends into New Week
commentary: Risk-on momentum carries into Asia session as Trump-Iran ceasefire talks hold—S&P futures steady at 6,590 (+1.3% from Monday close) while oil extends two-day collapse with WTI at $88.78, down 35% from crisis highs. Gold continues safe-haven unwind at $4,399 (-2%), capping its steepest weekly correction since 2013. Crypto catches the tailwind: BTC +3.7% at $70,682, ETH +4.4% at $2,154 as risk appetite returns. Dollar weakens to 99.12 on improved sentiment; all eyes on whether Iran's 48-hour response deadline passes without incident.

title: Overnight Futures: Oil Collapses as Iran De-escalation Continues
commentary: WTI craters 11% to $87.30, Brent -12% to $98.60 as Trump's five-day strike pause continues to defuse supply fears—biggest multi-day crude unwind since 2020. Gold slips 0.9% to $4,448 as safe-haven premium bleeds out; crypto rides lingering risk-on momentum with BTC +3.6% at $71,050, ETH +4.8% at $2,169. Fed's Goolsbee warns on inflation in 'fraught but intense' climate—markets watching whether de-escalation holds or Iran ultimatum reignites volatility.

title: US Close: Wall Street Caps Historic Rally, Iran Breakthrough Lifts Sentiment
commentary: S&P 500 closes at 6,605, up 1.5% in one of the sharpest single-day reversals this year as Trump-Iran diplomacy halts military escalation. Dow finishes +900 points at 46,416, Nasdaq +1.9% to 21,993. Oil craters 9% to $89.30 as strike pause defuses supply fears—biggest drop since 2022. Gold unwind continues with spot down 3.4% to $4,418; crypto rides risk-on wave with BTC at $71,100. Week ahead: All eyes on Iran's 48-hour ultimatum response. Fed speak resumes Tuesday.
